message:
refactor(contract): make security helpers opt-in rather than required

Removes _acfs_is_interactive from the contract requirements since security
functions are now explicitly opt-in via acfs_security_init().

## Problem

The contract previously required _acfs_is_interactive to be defined, which:
- Created a hard dependency on security.sh being sourced before any module
- Caused circular dependency issues when security.sh itself needed contract
- Made testing generated scripts more complex (needed full security stack)

## Solution

Security functions are now opt-in:
- Modules that need security verification call acfs_security_init() explicitly
- The contract only validates core installation infrastructure
- This matches the generated HEADER pattern which guards acfs_security_init

## Changes

contract.sh:
- Removed _acfs_is_interactive from the missing functions check

test_contract.sh:
- Added define_required_functions_no_security() stub helper
- Added test_security_helpers_optional() test case verifying the contract
  passes without security.sh helpers defined

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -51,12 +51,6 @@ acfs_require_contract() {
     if ! declare -f run_as_current_shell >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         missing+=("run_as_current_shell function")
     fi
-    # Check for security.sh functions (since we rely on them for interactive checks)
-    if ! declare -f _acfs_is_interactive >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-        # This is often sourced via install.sh -> detect_environment -> security.sh
-        # But verify it's actually there to avoid runtime errors in stack.sh/others
-        missing+=("_acfs_is_interactive (security.sh)")
-    fi
 
     if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
         if declare -f log_error >/dev/null 2>&1; then
